results.name: Danaher
results.key_finding: Night 7 Gen-2 forensic anchor: $172.5M shareholder securities settlement (April 22-23, 2026) — largest D.D.C. securities settlement since 1996; cluster invalidation continues.

results.name: General Mills
results.key_finding: Dual-trigger Gen-2 anchor: WARN Act violation (163 workers, April 7) + Davis v. General Mills racial-harassment class action survived dismissal (April 22 'one more chance' ruling).

results.name: Booz Allen Hamilton
results.key_finding: First pipeline capture: Treasury cancelled all 31 contracts ($21M) Jan 26, 2026 over Littlejohn IRS leak (406K taxpayers); April 2026 Maryland federal court refused to dismiss class action — sovereign-counterparty trust revocation.

results.name: Eli Lilly
results.key_finding: SCOTUS petition March 21, 2026 (No. 25-1126) seeking to declare FCA qui tam provisions unconstitutional — affirmative legal action to dismantle accountability mechanism is INT/ACC evidence per Palantir-2026-04-23 precedent; Streck $183M underlying judgment.

results.name: Finland
results.key_finding: Countries-ceiling Night 5: composite-100 ceiling artifact + April 16, 2026 deportation-enforcement and pre-emptive entry-ban proposal + Border Security Act extension to end-2026. Exemplary band maintained on substantive Kela infrastructure.

results.name: Denmark
results.key_finding: Countries-ceiling Night 5: composite-100 ceiling artifact + ECJ December 2025 ghetto-law ruling (potential ethnic-discrimination violation) + March 2026 visa ban for Afghanistan/Cameroon/Myanmar/Sudan + sustained Amnesty/UN-CAT non-refoulement criticism. Largest Nordic-trio correction.

results.name: Sweden
results.key_finding: Countries-ceiling Night 5: composite-97.2 ceiling artifact + April 17, 2026 'honest living' Aliens Act proposal — vague conduct-test creating two-tier residence-permit standard for migrants; companion proposal abolishes permanent permits for refugees.

results.name: OpenAI
results.key_finding: Musk dropped fraud claims April 25, 2026 — case narrows to unjust enrichment + breach of charitable trust; jury selection April 27. Charitable-trust claim directly addresses founding-mission abandonment. Hold confirmation pending Day 1 trial proceedings April 27.

results.name: xAI/Grok
results.key_finding: Floor-at-2.2 confirmation. SpaceX S-1 IPO filing (April 23) formally documents Grok investigations as material market-ban risk — escalates evidence quality (company-admitted) but published floor cannot express further downward movement. Methodology floor-escalation note retained.

results.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
results.key_finding: MONUSCO chief Swan April 25 briefing: conditions for effective ceasefire monitoring not met; ongoing fighting South Kivu; 6.4M displaced. April 22 correction stands; reaffirmation.

results.name: South Sudan
results.deferral_reason: UNMISS Security Council vote not yet held (mandate expires April 30, 5 days remain). Re-queue immediately on vote outcome.
results.key_finding: Deferred per Night 6 protocol. SSPDF-SPLA-IO hostilities at 2018-peak scale; vote watch active.

data_integrity_actions.description: Updated rotation-state.json: anthropic.composite 90.9 -> 61.6 and band exemplary -> established to match live ai-labs.json (post-2026-04-22 correction). Fourth consecutive scan flagging this; now resolved.

scanner_baseline_drift_notes: Scanner showed F500 Gen-2 cluster at composite 76.4; live fortune-500.json has cluster at 81.4. Used 81.4 as published baseline per critical pre-task instruction.
Scanner showed United States at 35.5; live countries.json has US at 25.0. US assessment deferred (insufficient April 22-25 window evidence to drive ≥5-point delta from 25.0 baseline).
Scanner showed Israel at 23.4; live countries.json has Israel at 8.8 — already deep-critical. Confirmation appropriate; no proposal.
methodology_notes: Sixth consecutive night of F500 Gen-2 cluster invalidation. Forensic anchors now: TIAA (April 23 -38.9), Masimo (April 23 -33.0), Becton Dickinson (April 24 -27.3), Danaher (April 25 -26.7), General Mills (April 25 -28.3), Eli Lilly (April 25 -25.1).

Cluster-sprint completion candidates remaining: Cummins, U.S. Bancorp, American Express. Cummins evidence base (January 2024 EPA settlement) is outside April 22-25 window; deferred per 'strong forensic anchor' instruction.

Countries-Ceiling Night 5 completed: Norway (-15.3), Iceland (-12.5), Luxembourg (-16.2), Finland (-15.6), Denmark (-18.7), Sweden (-15.9), Germany (-23.1), Netherlands (-21.5). Average ceiling-artifact delta: -17.4 across 8 corrections. Top-10 countries ceiling pattern fully audited except New Zealand.

Per Palantir-2026-04-23 precedent: Eli Lilly SCOTUS petition is cited as values-divergence evidence (affirmative anti-accountability legal action) — same scoring pattern as Palantir's 22-point manifesto.
Independence policy preserved in all 7 proposals: no commercial relationship factored into scoring; all evidence is publicly available primary or secondary sources.

Floor methodology limitation flagged for next index build: xAI/Grok at 2.2 cannot express SpaceX S-1 IPO risk-disclosure escalation within published-floor constraint. IMPROVEMENT_BACKLOG Tier H4 floor-escalation logic remains overdue.
